title: Lakshmi Chalisa
intro: Shri Lakshmi Chalisa - Hymn to Goddess Lakshmi
description:
॥ Shri Lakshmi Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥

Matu Lakshmi kari kripa, karo hriday mein vaas.
Manokamna siddh kari, paruvahu meri aas.

॥ Sortha ॥

Yahi mor ardas, haath jod vinti karun.
Sab vidhi karau suvaas, jai janani jagadambika.

॥ Chaupai ॥

Sindhu suta main sumirau tohi.
Gyan, buddhi, vidya do mohi.
Tum saman nahin koi upkari.
Sab vidhi puravahu aas hamari.
Jai jai jagat janani jagadamba.
Sabki tum hi ho avalamba.
Tum hi ho sab ghat ghat vasi.
Vinti yahi hamari khasi.
Jagjanani jai sindhu kumari.
Deenan ki tum ho hitkari.
Vinavaun nitya tumahin maharani.
Kripa karau jag janani bhavani.
Kehi vidhi stuti karaun tihari.
Sudhi lijai apradh bisari.
Kripa drishti chitvavo mam ori.
Jagjanani vinti sun mori.
Gyan buddhi jai sukh ki data.
Sankat haro hamari mata.
Ksheersindhu jab Vishnu mathayo.
Chaudah ratna sindhu mein payo.
Chaudah ratna mein tum sukhrasi.
Seva kiyo prabhu bani dasi.
Jab jab janm jahan prabhu linha.
Rup badal tahan seva kinha.
Swayam Vishnu jab nar tanu dhara.
Linheu avadhpuri avtara.
Tab tum pragat janakpur mahi.
Seva kiyo hriday pulakahi.
Apnaya tohi antaryami.
Vishva vidit tribhuvan ki swami.
Tum sam prabal shakti nahin ani.
Kahan lau mahima kahaun bakhani.
Man kram vachan karai sevakai.
Man ichchhit vanchhit phal pai.
Taji chhal kapat aur chaturai.
Pujahin vividh bhanti manlai.
Aur haal main kahaun bujhai.
Jo yah path karai man lai.
Tako koi kasht noi.
Man ichchhit pavai phal soi.
Trahi trahi jai duhkh nivarini.
Trividh taap bhav bandhan harini.
Jo chalisa padhai padhavai.
Dhyan lagakar sunai sunavai.
Takau koi na rog satavai.
Putra adi dhan sampatti pavai.
Putrahin aru sampati hina.
Andh badhir kodhi ati dina.
Vipr bolay kai path karavai.
Shanka dil mein kabhi na lavai.
Path karavai din chalisa.
Ta par kripa karain gaurisa.
Sukh sampatti bahut si pavai.
Kami nahin kahu ki aavai.
Barah maas karai jo puja.
Tehi sam dhanya aur nahin duja.
Pratidin path karai man mahi.
Un sam koi jag mein kahun nahin.
Bahuvidhi kya main karaun badai.
Ley pariksha dhyan lagai.
Kari vishwas karai vrat nema.
Hoy siddh upjai ur prema.
Jai jai jai Lakshmi bhavani.
Sab mein vyapit ho gun khani.
Tumharo tej prabal jag mahi.
Tum sam kou dayalu kahun nahin.
Mohi anath ki sudhi ab lijai.
Sankat kati bhakti mohi dijai.
Bhool chook kari kshama hamari.
Darshan dajai dasha nihari.
Bin darshan vyakul adhikari.
Tumahin achhat duhkh sehte bhari.
Nahin mohi gyan buddhi hai tan mein.
Sab jaanat ho apne man mein.
Rup chaturbhuj karke dharan.
Kasht mor ab karahu nivaran.
Kehi prakar main karaun badai.
Gyan buddhi mohi nahin adhikai.
Ramdas ab kahai pukari.
Karo door tum vipatti hamari.

॥ Doha ॥

Trahi trahi duhkh harini, haro vegi sab traas.
Jayati jayati jai Lakshmi, karo shatru ko naash.
Ramdas dhari dhyan nit, vinay karat kar jor.
Matu Lakshmi das par, karahu daya ki kor.
meaning:
॥ Shri Lakshmi Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥

Matu Lakshmi kari kripa, karo hriday mein vaas.
O Mother Lakshmi, show your grace and dwell in my heart.
Manokamna siddh kari, paruvahu meri aas.
Fulfill my desires and grant my hopes.

॥ Sortha ॥

Yahi mor ardas, haath jod vinti karun.
This is my prayer, I fold my hands and plead.
Sab vidhi karau suvaas, jai janani jagadambika.
Bless me in all ways, victory to you, Mother of the world.

॥ Chaupai ॥

Sindhu suta main sumirau tohi.
O daughter of the ocean, I remember you.
Gyan, buddhi, vidya do mohi.
Grant me knowledge, wisdom, and learning.
Tum saman nahin koi upkari.
There is no benefactor equal to you.
Sab vidhi puravahu aas hamari.
Fulfill my hopes in every way.
Jai jai jagat janani jagadamba.
Victory, victory to the Mother of the world, Jagadamba.
Sabki tum hi ho avalamba.
You are the only support of everyone.
Tum hi ho sab ghat ghat vasi.
You reside in the heart of every being.
Vinti yahi hamari khasi.
This is my special request.
Jagjanani jai sindhu kumari.
Victory to you, O Mother of the world, daughter of the ocean.
Deenan ki tum ho hitkari.
You are the well-wisher of the poor and distressed.
Vinavaun nitya tumahin maharani.
I bow to you daily, O Maharani.
Kripa karau jag janani bhavani.
Show your grace, O Mother Bhavani.
Kehi vidhi stuti karaun tihari.
In what way should I praise you?
Sudhi lijai apradh bisari.
Forget my sins and think of me.
Kripa drishti chitvavo mam ori.
Cast your merciful glance towards me.
Jagjanani vinti sun mori.
O Mother of the world, hear my prayer.
Gyan buddhi jai sukh ki data.
Victory to the giver of knowledge, wisdom, and happiness.
Sankat haro hamari mata.
O Mother, remove all my troubles.
Ksheersindhu jab Vishnu mathayo.
When Lord Vishnu churned the ocean of milk.
Chaudah ratna sindhu mein payo.
He found fourteen gems in the ocean.
Chaudah ratna mein tum sukhrasi.
Among those gems, you are the essence of happiness.
Seva kiyo prabhu bani dasi.
You served the Lord by becoming his handmaiden.
Jab jab janm jahan prabhu linha.
Whenever and wherever the Lord took birth.
Rup badal tahan seva kinha.
You changed your form and served him there.
Swayam Vishnu jab nar tanu dhara.
When Vishnu himself took human form.
Linheu avadhpuri avtara.
And took an avatar in Ayodhya.
Tab tum pragat janakpur mahi.
Then you appeared in Janakpur.
Seva kiyo hriday pulakahi.
You served him with a joyous heart.
Apnaya tohi antaryami.
The Omniscient Lord accepted you.
Vishva vidit tribhuvan ki swami.
You are known to the world as the Goddess of the three worlds.
Tum sam prabal shakti nahin ani.
There is no greater power than you.
Kahan lau mahima kahaun bakhani.
How far can I describe your glory?
Man kram vachan karai sevakai.
One who serves you by thought, action, and speech.
Man ichchhit vanchhit phal pai.
Receives the desired fruits of their heart.
Taji chhal kapat aur chaturai.
Leaving aside deceit, trickery, and cleverness.
Pujahin vividh bhanti manlai.
People worship you with devotion in various ways.
Aur haal main kahaun bujhai.
I explain another truth to you.
Jo yah path karai man lai.
Whoever recites this prayer with a focused mind.
Tako koi kasht noi.
They face no troubles.
Man ichchhit pavai phal soi.
They attain their desired fruits.
Trahi trahi jai duhkh nivarini.
Victory to you, who removes all sorrows.
Trividh taap bhav bandhan harini.
You destroy the three types of miseries and worldly bonds.
Jo chalisa padhai padhavai.
Whoever reads or gets this Chalisa read.
Dhyan lagakar sunai sunavai.
Whoever listens to it with focus.
Takau koi na rog satavai.
No disease will torment them.
Putra adi dhan sampatti pavai.
They will attain wealth, prosperity, and progeny.
Putrahin aru sampati hina.
Those who are childless or lacking wealth.
Andh badhir kodhi ati dina.
The blind, deaf, lepers, and the destitute.
Vipr bolay kai path karavai.
Should call a Brahmin and have this path recited.
Shanka dil mein kabhi na lavai.
Never harbor any doubt in your heart.
Path karavai din chalisa.
Have the prayer recited for forty days.
Ta par kripa karain gaurisa.
Lord Shiva (Gaurisa) will grace them.
Sukh sampatti bahut si pavai.
They will attain great wealth and happiness.
Kami nahin kahu ki aavai.
They will never lack anything.
Barah maas karai jo puja.
Whoever worships you throughout the twelve months.
Tehi sam dhanya aur nahin duja.
There is no one as blessed as them.
Pratidin path karai man mahi.
Those who recite this daily in their hearts.
Un sam koi jag mein kahun nahin.
There is no one in the world equal to them.
Bahuvidh kya main karaun badai.
How much more should I praise you?
Ley pariksha dhyan lagai.
Test it by focusing your meditation.
Kari vishwas karai vrat nema.
Faithfully observe the vows and rules.
Hoy siddh upjai ur prema.
Your wishes will be fulfilled, and love will arise in your heart.
Jai jai jai Lakshmi bhavani.
Victory, victory, victory to Mother Lakshmi Bhavani.
Sab mein vyapit ho gun khani.
You are the treasure of virtues present in everyone.
Tumharo tej prabal jag mahi.
Your radiance is powerful in this world.
Tum sam kou dayalu kahun nahin.
There is no one as merciful as you anywhere.
Mohi anath ki sudhi ab lijai.
Now take notice of me, an orphan.
Sankat kati bhakti mohi dijai.
Cut my troubles and grant me devotion.
Bhool chook kari kshama hamari.
Forgive all my mistakes.
Darshan dajai dasha nihari.
Looking at my state, grant me your vision.
Bin darshan vyakul adhikari.
I am distressed and yearning for your vision.
Tumahin achhat duhkh sehte bhari.
Even with you present, I am enduring great sorrow.
Nahin mohi gyan buddhi hai tan mein.
I have no wisdom or intellect in my body.
Sab jaanat ho apne man mein.
You know everything within your own heart.
Rup chaturbhuj karke dharan.
By assuming your four-armed form.
Kasht mor ab karahu nivaran.
Now remove my distress.
Kehi prakar main karaun badai.
In what way should I praise you?
Gyan buddhi mohi nahin adhikai.
I do not have much wisdom or intellect.
Ramdas ab kahai pukari.
Ramdas now proclaims aloud.
Karo door tum vipatti hamari.
Please remove all my calamities.

॥ Doha ॥

Trahi trahi duhkh harini, haro vegi sab traas.
O remover of sorrows, save me and quickly remove all fears.
Jayati jayati jai Lakshmi, karo shatru ko naash.
Victory, victory to Lakshmi, destroy all my enemies.
Ramdas dhari dhyan nit, vinay karat kar jor.
Ramdas meditates daily and prays with folded hands.
Matu Lakshmi das par, karahu daya ki kor.
O Mother Lakshmi, cast your merciful glance upon your servant.

faq.q1: What is the Lakshmi Chalisa and what is its significance?
faq.a1: The Lakshmi Chalisa is a sacred hymn consisting of 40 verses dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi, the Hindu deity of wealth, fortune, prosperity, and light. Reciting this Chalisa is a powerful way to seek her blessings, attain financial stability, and invite peace and harmony into one's home.
faq.q2: What are the main benefits of reciting the Lakshmi Chalisa?
faq.a2:
Regular recitation is believed to offer several spiritual and material benefits:
Wealth and Prosperity: It is widely considered helpful in removing financial obstacles and opening new avenues of growth.
Mental Peace: It instills a sense of positivity, reducing anxiety and stress.
Good Fortune: It is believed to bring happiness, luck, and overall well-being to the household.
Relief from Debts: Devotees believe that sincere recitation helps in overcoming financial burdens and debts.
faq.q3: What is the best day and time to recite the Lakshmi Chalisa?
faq.a3: Friday is considered the most auspicious day dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi. While you can recite it any day, reading it during the evening on a Friday, perhaps while lighting a ghee lamp, is considered highly effective.
faq.q4: Are there any specific rituals required to recite it?
faq.a4: Goddess Lakshmi appreciates purity and simplicity. There are no complex rituals; simply sit before an idol or picture of the Goddess in a clean, quiet space. The most important requirement is a pure heart and a sincere intention.
faq.q5: Do I need a Guru to recite the Lakshmi Chalisa?
faq.a5: No, you do not need a formal Guru or initiation to recite the Lakshmi Chalisa. Maa Lakshmi listens to the heartfelt prayers of all her children. Your devotion is the only prerequisite needed to establish a connection with her.
